title: The Umbra
wikitext:

{{DISPLAYTITLE:The Umbra}} {{Notice|body=It is HEAVILY recommended that you use dark mode when reading this page, as it's meant to have a shadowy theme.}}
'''The Umbra''' [[File:IneternaLine.png|400px|center|link= ]]
{{Audio|file=Shadow Beneath A Mask.mp3|volume=0.4}} [[File:The Umbra's Mask.jpg|center|350px|link= ]]
On your lowest day, he came to you with an offer.
He offered you a mask.
At first, you hesitated.
Who wouldn't? Every moment of that decision remains deeply engraved in your mind.
But you couldn't bear to see what you had become after that horrible incident.
So you took it.
It shouldn't have to be this complicated. But everything here is complicated.
And once you wear the mask, the challenge is not to keep wearing it but to take it off.
It's an odd feeling, to say the least. You run on desperation, on vengeance, and forget what the mask is truly meant to obscure.
A twisted purpose.
But that's now a quaint issue.
He tells you the script. What's bound to happen.
You'd scream. You'd say that's not real. But now, you can only stare.
''And wait for doomsday''.
[[File:Doomsdayclocks.jpg|center|350px|link= ]]
By the time the clock struck, you had changed.
How could one live with this hell? This beckoning? You had no motivation to figure that out. No motivation to do anything at all, in fact.
But you now had to do your part of the deal. Your new life. Your new view.
The next moment, you were in a new world. A crumbling one.
The wanderers scurried, panicked, ''yelled''.
But why weren't you scared?
What happened to your sentimentality? Your diligence? Is it too, hidden by the mask?
No matter. You were on duty.
You'd approach one of the yelling wanderers, offering them "help", or at least, that's what you thought.
A safe place. One that even the darkness cannot touch.
You knew it was a lie. A stage. But you didn't care. Not like you used to. For now, you are the one performing.
The talk felt like it was eons long. Eventually, the wanderer fell for the trick. The fate that awaited them was now sealed.
And now? You'd continue this same farce.
''Wanderer by wanderer.''
[[File:Dominofall.png|center|350px|link= ]]
But it wouldn't be this simple. You knew it.
You figured that when suddenly, he called for you again.
Another order? It almost felt like you were playing his puppet.
There were 4 new people you now had to keep your eye on.
Ones defying his script.
''Two souls whose minds unstained wander into chaos—''
''And another two who, in pursuit of repentance, seek to turn the tide.''
How interesting.
You were confused. People were still trying to push back against a force beyond the reaches of humanity.
But who were you to say that?
Were you ''still'' a human?
You can't quite answer that yet.
''And The Orchestrator wants his jobs done now and well.''
[[File:PullingTheStrings.jpg|center|350px|link= ]]
It had been a long day. A lifetime's worth of watching the gazes of the panicked wanderers as you met them.
You retreated into whatever place could give you a lure of safety in this world.
As you took in your new surroundings, you could only ask yourself one thing.
''Is this what was destined for you?''
Before, you'd worry. Now, you see them all as pathetic little ants fighting for their lives.
Is this what was destined for you? To lose your very humanity?
No. That couldn't be true.
You stumbled. You could only do what was possible to humor your worthless denial.
But when you stared at the window, you finally met with ''the mask''.
You remembered the deal you made.
He said the knowledge would help you survive with the fatal condition you had.
But there was a raw, bitter part of the deal you only now realize.
You are in places in which you shouldn't be.
You are involved in things you cannot control.
You are a cog in his machine.
''You know things that you shouldn't.''
The curse that was tearing you apart was gone, but now a new rot had appeared.
One that ate at you from the inside, crept into the edges of your vision.
You thought of calling for help.
But the mask covered anything lying beneath.
You were now but a shadow of your former self. '''''An umbra.'''''
{{User:AstralRose555/quote|fontsize=23|And the Lord God commanded the man, saying, Of every tree of the garden thou mayest freely eat: But of the tree of the knowledge of good and evil, thou shalt not eat of it: for in the day that thou eatest thereof thou shalt surely die.|Genesis 2:16–17|color=#995cfc}}
[[File:IneternaLineFlipped.png|400px|center|link= ]]
[[File:Vignette.png|2000px]]
{{Collapse|num=1|initial=
'''A face obscured by shadow...'''
|final=
'''''Had now become the shadow...'''''{{Underline|320e6d}}
|body=
Case Study Analysis: 13-A Recently, there had been multiple reports from wanderers who claim to have seen a strange, shadowy figure stalking from the darkness. From the few reports we've managed to gather, said figure appears to have some kind of purplish tint/hue surrounding them. This strange, new entity, due to its shadowy, dark appearance, has been nicknamed, '''The Umbra'''. From observations, '''The Umbra''' appears to be a humanoid creature with long hair, a torn black scarf, and a purple coat with a unique symbol on it. They appear to possess black gloves and dress shoes, and their face is mostly obscured by shadow. However, the most prevalent observation from most reports involved a mysterious purple opera mask, seemingly glued to its face. The mask appears to conceal some severe deformities caused by incomplete [[Nullification]], to which the entity had somehow survived. Multiple witnesses report acute distress during an encounter with '''The Umbra'''. Such reactions include shortness of breath and panic, disorientation and disassociation, and persistent hypervigilance in low-light environments. Exposure to the entity may also worsen already existing psychological conditions and induce sever stress responses in vulnerable individuals. Due to the high psychological risk, as well as the entities unpredictable behavior, wanderers are strongly advised to avoid all contact with '''The Umbra'''. Though its exact goals remain unclear, multiple reports suggest a calculated, manipulated nature, rather than just random hostility.
You think you left them behind. You think distance is enough. But shadows are patient, and masks don't forget Long after you run, he will still linger. Not in the halls, but in your memory. '''In the corners where your light never reaches.'''
Testimony Report ██
Date: ██/██/24 For all purposes, the date of occurrence and name of the interviewer have been classified. Please avoid discussion of this document without proper notice. {{Underline|320e6d}} ???: Alright. Mister.. Michael, was it? Michael: Yeah, that's me. ???: Excellent, then we may begin. Now tell me, what level were you in when this whole.. incident happened? Michael: I don't know.. since I found this base, it's been all foggy. I think it was some sort of indoor level, grey hallways, pretty empty, that sort of thing. ???: Possibly Level █. And what were you doing at the time? Michael: Wandering. Like most do. I had a flashlight, and a small knife. I was on edge, as I had a close call with a [[Entity 8|Hound]] earlier. So I rounded a corner, checked the wall, looked both ways… ???: [Writing on notepad] Wandering.. corner... go on. Michael: And that's when I saw it.. ???: Ah. Can you describe it? Michael: It looked like a figure. But it wasn't like a [[Entity 10|Skin Stealer]] or any of those. It was shadowy, and had some purple glow surrounding them. ???: A purple glow... I see. Did you perhaps notice any features? Michael: Not much. It was too far off. Maybe I just didn't look hard enough, or I forgot some stuff. But I swear, I saw a mask. Just half of one. Purple, or something? It looked like one of those you'd see in a play or one of those old opera shows. ???: A mask... and you are *certain* of this? Michael: As certain as I can be. I mean, the longer I looked at this... "entity", the less certain I was of anything. I could have sworn that under the mask, their face looked.. [[Nullification|glitchy]]. Almost as if it wasn't fully there. ???: ''Glitchy'', you say? [Pauses, writing] Fascinating... Michael: Yeah. The rest of their face was obscured by the shadow. I didn't really remember anything else. ???: [Scribbles in notes] And what happened next? Michael: [Shifts in chair] At first, I just froze. I couldn't make sense of any of it. Then, when I looked away for a brief second, I look back, and the figure shifted closer. Like it teleported when I wasn't looking. ???: Or, that ''glitching'' thing you mentioned, could be a result of that. Michael: Whatever the reason, I couldn't make heads or tails of it. But then they moved closer. Just standing there, staring directly at me. ???: [Shifts in chair, voice tightening] Staring... ''directly''? Michael: Yeah. I could hardly see the part of their face obscured by the mask, and the other by shadow, but it still felt as if they were making direct eye contact. ???: [Furrows brow, looking at notes] This is... interesting, to say the least. Michael: I agree. ???: Now this "entity", did it attempt to communicate? Michael: [Hesitates] I.. I thought I heard something. A voice, to which it felt as if it was inside my head. ???: And what did it say? Michael: Something about this ''safe place'', that it could take me from here, that I didn't need to run anymore. ???: And did you accept this offer? Michael: Hell no! I grabbed my stuff and bolted. I didn't care where. That ''thing'' never ran after me, but I swear I could feel it watching me the entire time. ???: [Pondering] Yet you are still here. Alive, and not in that ''place'', which means they allowed you to leave. Curious, indeed. Michael: [Uneasy] Allowed me..? ???: Yes, allowed you. If it wished you gone, if it pursued, you would not be here recounting this information. Perhaps it didn't feel as if you were "worthy". Or maybe it felt a tinge of mercy for once. Michael: [Visibly shaken] Mercy? I saw what that ''thing'' looked like, it didn't feel ''human'' enough for mercy! ???: [Leans back, smiling faintly] Then consider yourself fortunate Michael. For whatever reason, the mask looked away from you. Michael: The mask looked away...
[End of transcript]
{{Underline|320e6d}} Addendum
Following debriefing, subject Michael was relocated to Base Equilibriums Observation Wing for 48 hours. Though they appear physically unharmed, subject retains alarmingly fast heartbeat and breathing rates, indicating acute stress responses. They appear to exhibit flashbacks when encountering shadows or low light areas. '''Final Note''': The entity appears to exhibit selective engagement, allowing certain people to escape, while also inducing prolonged psychological effects. {{Underline|#320e6d}} [[File:Umbravculogo.png|center|320px]]
'''THE TRINITY MUST CONTINUE.'''
Four names. Two that wander unsuspectingly into chaos. And another two, who desperately search for a solution. These are the souls now under his gaze. Under ''my'' gaze. You imagine that they may change the order of events. You imagine that they can change fate, naive. It's not that I try to prevent this. But ask.. truly ask: Why strive to alter a future that has been set in stone, ever since existence itself took their first breath? Anything with a beginning must meet its end, as I always say. There's no point in trying to rewrite fate. The story is already written. Yet still... there is another. One who narrates their own story, separate, yet untethered. A story that splinters off, a witness to the ends of what is, and what will be. They see, they survive, and interpret. But even they aren't free. Just another survivor in the midst of this madness, who has simply come to terms with the end. The four will continue their paths. Two will meet the face of shadows, and the other two will continue to falter in search of a light that never existed. The mask will reveal itself. {{Collapse|num=3|initial=
'''For it is waiting...'''
|final=
'''''As it always has..'''''{{Underline|FFFFFF}}
